name: Submittable Grant and Application Management API
what_it_does: Submittable cloud grant and application management REST API for foundations, nonprofits, corporations, and government agencies to manage grant applications, scholarship programs, and social impact initiatives. Enables AI agents to manage submission form and application program creation for grant program workflow automation, handle application review and scoring workflow for selection committee automation, access eligibility screening and requirement validation for application qualification automation, retrieve award and decision notification for grant outcome communication automation, manage reviewer assignment and evaluation coordination for grant committee management automation, handle file upload and document collection for applicant document management automation, access program analytics and submission reporting for grant portfolio performance automation, retrieve payment and disbursement workflow for award fulfillment automation, manage multi-round selection process for competitive grant program automation, and integrate Submittable with CRM, accounting, and impact measurement systems for end-to-end grant program management.
use_cases: Processing grant applications from foundation program management agents
Managing scholarship reviews from selection committee automation agents
Tracking social impact awards from corporate giving automation agents
Reporting program outcomes from grant analytics agents
not_for: Large foundation portfolio management (use Fluxx or Blackbaud Grantseeker)
best_when: A foundation, nonprofit, corporation, or government agency wanting AI agents to automate grant application intake, reviewer assignment, eligibility screening, award decisions, and payment disbursement within Submittable's application management platform.
avoid_when: EQUAL OPPORTUNITY COMPLIANCE FOR SCHOLARSHIP PROGRAMS: Automated scholarship application scoring via Submittable using AI must comply with equal opportunity laws; automated scoring models that correlate with protected characteristics (race, gender, disability) in scholarship selection create Title VI and Title IX compliance exposure. GRANT PROGRAM TERMS PUBLIC NOTICE REQUIREMENTS: Government agency grant programs managed via Submittable must comply with public notice requirements for competitive grant awards; automated launch of government grant programs without required public notice period and publication creates administrative law compliance violation. APPLICANT DATA CONFIDENTIALITY FOR COMPETING APPLICANTS: Automated application review processes via Submittable must enforce reviewer access controls; automated reviewer access to competing applicant submissions without blind review configuration creates unfair competition in grant selection.

auth.notes: Submittable uses API key and OAuth for integrations. REST API with JSON. Missoula, Montana HQ. Founded 2010 by Michael FitzGerald. Private (~$30M raised). Application management for nonprofits, foundations, corporations, government. Creative writing, grants, scholarships, social impact programs. Bonterra (formerly Social Solutions + EveryAction + CyberGrants) ecosystem partner. SOC2. Competes with Fluxx, Foundant, and Blackbaud for grant program management.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — per-program or annual subscription for organizations
pricing.paid_tiers: Per-program flat fee; annual subscription for multi-program organizations; enterprise pricing for large foundations and corporations
pricing.notes: Missoula MT. Private. Founded 2010. Per-program and annual subscription. Enterprise for high-volume. No free tier.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API key; application and review queries throttled; webhooks for submission and decision events

known_agent_gotchas: REVIEWER ASSIGNMENT CONFLICT OF INTEREST BLIND REVIEW: Automated reviewer assignment in competitive grant programs must implement blind review configuration for programs requiring anonymized applicant review; automated assignment without blind review removes reviewer from competition program may expose reviewer to applicant identity in programs requiring blind selection

SUBMISSION DEADLINE TIMEZONE ACCURACY: Submittable application deadline enforcement applies at submission timestamp in configured timezone; automated submission deadline notifications must account for applicant timezone relative to program deadline timezone; deadline communications without timezone clarification create applicant confusion and potential deadline dispute

WEBHOOK DUPLICATE SUBMISSION EVENT HANDLING: Submittable may deliver duplicate webhook events for submission and review status changes under high load; automated downstream processes triggered by Submittable webhooks must implement idempotency on submission ID and event type to prevent duplicate application processing and duplicate decision notifications

DECISION NOTIFICATION BEFORE REVIEW COMPLETE: Automated decision notification via Submittable API triggered on initial review score without final committee approval creates premature decision disclosure; implement committee approval gate before automated decision notification to applicants

MULTI-ROUND SELECTION APPLICANT DATA CARRY-FORWARD: Automated multi-round selection processes via Submittable must correctly carry forward applicant data between rounds; automated round 2 reviewer assignment without round 1 score data propagation creates incomplete applicant record for downstream reviewers

GOVERNMENT PROGRAM PUBLIC NOTICE PUBLICATION GATE: Government agency grant programs via Submittable require public notice publication before application window opens; automated program launch without public notice confirmation creates administrative law due process violation; implement publication confirmation gate for government agency automated program launch
agent_readiness.security_notes: Grant and application management. SOC2. API key/OAuth. US. Applicant personal data, grant program information, and selection data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Application management. SOC2. API key/OAuth. US. Applicant PII, program information, and selection data.
